Prunus serrulata has been traditionally used in East Asian medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce stress, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression. The bioactive constituents of Prunus serrulata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Prunus serrulata is often used to make tea, wine, and other preparations, such as dried flowers and bark, which are used to treat various health conditions.
